links.targetTitle: The adapter encrypts at the app layer with AES-256-GCM derived from the user's wallet signature

global.title: Connect derives a deterministic AES-256-GCM key from a one-time wallet signature, caches it, and encrypts every operation batch before Swarm upload.
global.content:
## Key derivation (one-time)

```
personal_sign("Sign to derive Swarm key...")
  -> keccak256(signature)
  -> 32-byte private key
  -> cached in IndexedDB  (no repeated signing)
```

Because `personal_sign` is deterministic per message, **same wallet = same key = same access**. Recovery on a new device only needs the wallet.

## Operation batch encryption

Every batch uploaded to `/bytes` is AES-256-GCM encrypted before upload and decrypted after download. Payload format:

```
[SWE prefix (3B)] [IV (12B)] [ciphertext]
```

The SWE prefix is a magic-byte marker that lets the client reject any payload it accidentally reads that was not produced by this pipeline.

## Feed authentication

Feeds are signed with the same wallet-derived Swarm private key — only the owner can write, but anyone with the owner's public key (i.e. anyone who knows the Ethereum address) can read.

## Where it lives

- `adapter/src/swarm-crypto.ts` — AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt.
- `adapter/src/wallet-signer.ts` — wallet signature to key derivation.
global.description: personal_sign with a fixed message yields a keccak256 key cached in IndexedDB; every /bytes upload is AES-256-GCM with format SWE-prefix || IV(12) || ciphertext.
